content.problemStatement:
For a sequence BBB, the MEX (minimum excluded value) of BBB, denoted as MEX(B)MEX(B)MEX(B), is the smallest non-negative integer that does not appear in BBB.

You are given two integers NNN and KKK.

Consider all permutations PPP of the integers {0,1,...,N−10, 1, ..., N-10,1,...,N−1}.

For a permutation PPP, define a set S(P)S(P)S(P) containing all distinct MEX values of all its subarrays.

Formally,

S(P)S(P)S(P) = { MEX(P[l..r])∣1≤l≤r≤NMEX(P[l..r]) | 1 ≤ l ≤ r ≤ N MEX(P[l..r])∣1≤l≤r≤N}

Your task is to count how many permutations PPP of {0,1,...,N−10, 1, ..., N-10,1,...,N−1} satisfy ∣S(P)∣=K|S(P)| = K∣S(P)∣=K, where ∣S(P)∣|S(P)|∣S(P)∣ denotes the number of elements in S(P)S(P)S(P) (the size/cardinality of the set).

Since the answer can be large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line containing two integers NNN and KKK - the size of the permutation and required size of the MEX set.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid permutations PPP for which the size of the MEX set is exactly KKK, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1035\cdot10^35⋅103.
